extraction.one_line_summary: KUSS Sepp won from the breakaway after JUMBO-VISMA successfully placed multiple riders forward while UAE failed to control despite POGACAR holding yellow comfortably
tactical_patterns: MATTHEWS Michael made the 25-rider break despite being sprinter, targeting intermediate sprint points while 24min down overall.
JUMBO-VISMA placed KUSS and VAN AERT in 25-rider break, converting domestique depth into stage wins when GC not threatened.
POGACAR absorbed repeated attacks on final climb by sitting on wheels then leading over summit to control descent.

rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: Won stage after showing Tour of Utah 2018 form pattern, third stage 15 Grand Tour win suggests targeted peaking
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Switched from worker to leader role mid-stage to set up KUSS, demonstrates tactical flexibility beyond pure domestique
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: neutral: Controlled attacks without difficulty but showed conservative descent tactics, prioritizing yellow retention over attacking
rider_signals.WOODS Mike: negative: Lost polka dots and visibly frustrated at finish, seventh KOM jersey change indicates vulnerability in early-stage positioning
